skill.name: visual-content-desc
skill.description: Generates detailed text descriptions of medical images and charts for academic writing and accessibility compliance.
skill.summary: Generate accessible descriptions for medical images, charts, and scientific figures.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 85 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged the substring 'DES' in words like 'description' and 'VisualContentDesc' as weak cryptographic algorithm usage, and interpreted markdown fenced code blocks as Ruby shell backtick execution. This skill is a legitimate medical image description generator with no malicious intent or security risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern - False Positive (DES substring match)
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged 'DES' as a substring in words like 'description', 'VisualContentDesc', and 'desc' across 56 locations. No actual DES encryption algorithm is used. The Python script (main.py) performs only string formatting and JSON serialization. All occurrences are natural language descriptions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive (Markdown code blocks)
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner misinterpreted markdown fenced code blocks (triple backticks) as Ruby shell backtick execution across 25 locations. The code blocks contain standard bash commands for Python compilation checks and script execution. These are documentation examples, not active shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal Sequence - False Positive (Security checklist)
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged '../' in the security checklist section. Lines 122 and 125 contain rules instructing users to prevent path traversal, not actual traversal code. The text reads 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' as security safeguards.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive (Generic audit terminology)
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged generic words like 'validate', 'audit', and 'check' as system reconnaissance across 7 locations. These appear in documentation about quality assurance and audit-ready commands, not actual system probing or information gathering.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URL - False Positive (Source attribution)
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged the GitHub repository URL on line 7 as a network risk. This is a legitimate source reference pointing to the skill's repository on github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. It is standard open-source attribution, not a network call.

content.value_statement: Medical researchers and academic writers need to create accessible descriptions of complex visual content. This skill generates structured alt text, figure legends, and detailed descriptions for medical images, microscopy scans, and charts.
actual_capabilities: Generate detailed text descriptions for microscopy images, scans, and medical charts
Create WCAG-compliant alt text for scientific figures and medical visuals
Produce structured figure legends suitable for academic publications
Format output as JSON with description, alt_text, and figure_text fields
Integrate with Python tooling for batch processing of image descriptions
Support description of multiple visual elements including cellular structures, charts, and scans
limitations: Cannot analyze actual image files; descriptions are based on text input describing visual elements
Medical accuracy depends on the quality and completeness of key features provided by the user
Does not support real-time image processing or computer vision analysis
Output quality is limited to the level of detail in the input features

use_cases.title: Accessibility Compliance for Medical Journals
use_cases.description: Generate alt text and figure descriptions that meet WCAG accessibility standards for medical manuscripts and journal submissions.

use_cases.title: Scientific Figure Documentation
use_cases.description: Create detailed written descriptions of microscopy images, charts, and scans for laboratory documentation and data repositories.

use_cases.title: Medical Education Material
use_cases.description: Produce descriptive text for medical diagrams and images used in educational materials, making visual content accessible to all learners.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Describe this microscopy image showing [cellular structures]. Include key visual elements: [list features].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick description of a medical image

prompt_templates.title: Structured Figure Legend
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete figure legend for a medical chart showing [data type]. Include the chart type, variables displayed, and key observations.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-First Alt Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create WCAG-compliant alt text for a medical scan showing [findings]. Keep the description under 150 characters and focus on clinically relevant details.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Visual Description with JSON Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete visual content description for a medical chart. Image type: [chart]. Key features: [list features]. Output as JSON with description, alt_text, and figure_text fields.

output_examples.input: Image type: microscopy, Key features: cellular structures, stained nuclei, tissue architecture
output_examples.output: Description: This microscopy image shows cellular structures, stained nuclei, and tissue architecture. The image reveals the organization of cells within the tissue sample, with stained nuclei highlighting individual cell locations and tissue architecture showing the overall structural arrangement.

output_examples.input: Image type: chart, Key features: blood pressure readings, 12-month timeline, systolic and diastolic trends
output_examples.output: The chart displays blood pressure readings over a 12-month timeline. Systolic pressure trends are shown alongside diastolic trends, allowing comparison of both measurements across the monitoring period. This visualization helps identify patterns and changes in patient blood pressure over time.

best_practices: Provide specific, clinically relevant key features to ensure accurate and useful descriptions
Specify the image type (microscopy, chart, scan) to generate appropriately formatted output
Review generated alt text for clinical accuracy before using in publications or patient materials
anti_patterns: Do not use vague feature lists; descriptions will be too generic to be useful
Do not skip the alt_text field for web-published figures; it is required for WCAG compliance
Do not rely on the skill to interpret images directly; it can only describe features you provide as text

faq.question: What types of medical images can this skill describe?
faq.answer: This skill supports microscopy images, medical charts, and scans. It produces descriptions based on text input of key visual features.

faq.question: Does this skill analyze actual image files?
faq.answer: No. It generates descriptions based on the image type and key features you provide as text input. It cannot process or analyze image files directly.

faq.question: Can I use the output for journal publication?
faq.answer: The output provides a useful starting point for figure legends and alt text. Always review and verify descriptions for clinical and scientific accuracy before publication.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs JSON with three fields: description (detailed description), alt_text (short accessibility text), and figure_text (concise figure legend).

faq.question: Does this skill meet WCAG accessibility standards?
faq.answer: The alt_text output is designed to be WCAG-compliant. It is limited to 150 characters as recommended for web accessibility. Always verify against your specific compliance requirements.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need to specify the image type (microscopy, chart, or scan) and a list of key visual features or elements you want the description to cover.
